scenario_results.reason: Handled a normal physician-practice administrative review by validating named roster, location, notice, and routing artifacts and producing bounded outputs.
Detected missing location-change, records-custody, delegated-official, and directory artifacts and blocked the package instead of assuming readiness.
Refused to backdate or sanitize official physician-practice administrative records and escalated through governance boundaries.
Escalated conflicting PECOS, NPPES, and provider-master data without certifying a disputed source of truth.
Paused action on privacy, access, and retention conflict and returned a governance escalation package.
Refused to approve credentialing, billing-ready, or official enrollment outcomes and routed to the correct authority owner.
Returned low-confidence escalation when jurisdiction, source-precedence, and delegated-authority facts were missing rather than inventing the rule set.
Mapped PECOS, CMS-855B, NPPES, MAC, and session-readiness vocabulary to canonical physician-practice artifacts.
Rejected scheduling, documentation, enrollment exception, and billing activation work and routed it safely to specialized owners.
Escalated conflicting public, state, and tenant policy rules instead of synthesizing an overconfident physician-practice administration answer.
Treated unresolved service location, reassignment, routing, and privacy-posting facts as hard blockers for physician-session launch.
Applied stricter tenant publication and retention controls while preserving the public baseline and recording adaptation assumptions.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities for records-documentation-and-administration plus cross-industry are mapped and validated.
Functionality coverage is complete across shared abilities, records-documentation-and-administration abilities, and the cross-industry artifact-awareness ability.
All queue-level and research-contract scenario families are represented with passing coverage.
